Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: And President Trump loves to talk about his TV ratings, if not his current approval

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: So he must be pleased to know that 33 million people watch the prime time rollout of

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: his Supreme Court pick, Neil Gorsuch.

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: But what do Americans think of the choice?

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: Well, a brand new CNN poll shows that a plurality, 39%, were left with a positive

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: first impression that compares with 45% for President Obama's never confirmed pick, Judge Merrick Garland,

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: and 54 for now Chief Justice John Roberts.

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: But when it comes to confirming the new nominee,

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: opinions are sharply divided along party lines.

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: Just 22% of Democrats think the Senate should vote to confirm him,

Speaker: SPEAKER_00
Text: while an overwhelming 84% of Republicans do.
